seo.title: Luma Ray 3.2 Modify Video, Reframe, Pricing and Examples | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use Luma Ray 3.2 in MaxVideoAI for source-video Modify, AI video Reframe, keyframed guide frames, silent 5s/10s video, prompts, examples and pricing.

overview: Luma Ray 3.2 is best positioned in MaxVideoAI as an editing-first Luma video route: Modify an existing source clip, steer selected moments with guide frames or keyframes, and Reframe approved footage into new aspect ratios. Text-to-video and image-to-video remain available for short Luma motion tests, but Ray 3.2 does not generate native audio or lip sync.
pricingNotes: All Ray 3.2 routes keep the MaxVideoAI customer quote aligned to the Fal reference price plus the configured margin, including Modify and Reframe. Internal routing does not change the client-facing price shown before generation.

hero.title: Luma Ray 3.2 - Modify and Reframe for current Luma video
hero.intro: Start from a source video when timing already works, then use Ray 3.2 Modify to change the look or guided moments, or Reframe to create vertical, square, landscape and ultrawide delivery cuts. Prompt-only and start-image generation stay available for short silent motion tests.
hero.badge: Modify video + Reframe · Guide/keyframes · Silent output

items.title: Vertical, square, wide or ultrawide Reframe cuts
Short silent Luma motion tests from text or stills
1080p inspection passes for selected edit candidates

technicalOverview.label: Workflows
technicalOverview.body: Source-video Modify, Reframe, text-to-video and image-to-video

technicalOverview.label: Duration
technicalOverview.body: 5s or 10s generation; source-video intake up to the configured clip limit for Modify/Reframe

technicalOverview.label: Aspect ratios
technicalOverview.body: Common wide, square, portrait and extended ratios exposed by the active route

technicalOverview.label: Guides
technicalOverview.body: One start image for image-to-video, optional guide frame or up to 64 indexed keyframes for Modify

technicalOverview.label: Source video
technicalOverview.body: One MP4, MOV or WebM source clip for Modify and Reframe

technicalOverview.label: Pricing
technicalOverview.body: Generation, Modify and Reframe use the same Fal-reference customer quote logic with MaxVideoAI margin

promptStructure.title: Prompt mini-guide
promptStructure.quote: Source goal + what stays + what changes + guide moment or target canvas + duration/resolution when relevant.
promptStructure.description: Ray 3.2 responds best when the mode is explicit. For Modify, state what should remain from the source video before describing the visual change. For Reframe, name the priority subject and how the new canvas should be filled.
steps: Start with Modify or Reframe when an approved source clip already exists.
Name what must remain from the source video: timing, camera path, subject motion or product scale.
Then describe the visual change, guide moment or target delivery ratio.
Choose 5s for tight edit checks or 10s for slower generated or modified reveals.
Use 540p or 720p for exploration and 1080p for selected checks.
Use prompt-only or start-image generation when there is no source clip yet.
Plan audio, voice and sound design outside Ray 3.2.

tips.title: Tips and limits
items: Use Modify when source timing already works and the look, subject detail or scene treatment needs to change.
Use guide frames or keyframes when specific source moments need visual direction.
Use Reframe when an approved master needs a new delivery ratio without starting over.
Keep generation prompts inside 5s or 10s and 540p to 1080p.
Use a start image when product shape, identity or composition matters but no source video exists yet.
Treat Ray 3.2 as silent video output and add audio later.
Review the exact price shown in the app before generation.

prompts.title: Modify source video
prompts.prompt: Keep the original camera move and timing from the source video, change the room into a bright editorial showroom, preserve product scale and subject motion, 5s, 720p.

prompts.title: Keyframed edit pass
prompts.prompt: Preserve the walking pace and camera dolly from the source clip. At the marked keyframes, shift the jacket from black nylon to cream technical fabric while keeping face, hands and background motion stable.

prompts.title: Reframe delivery cut
prompts.prompt: Reframe the source surfing clip to 9:16, keep the rider centered, extend the wave and sky naturally, preserve the original action timing.

prompts.title: Supporting generation test
prompts.prompt: Short silent product motion test: slow push-in on a premium speaker on a dark stone plinth, controlled reflections, soft rim light, subtle fabric vibration, 5s, 16:9, 720p.

faqs.q: What does Luma Ray 3.2 support in MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: The model page focuses on source-video Modify, Reframe, guide/keyframe editing, plus short text-to-video and image-to-video when you need a new silent Luma clip.

faqs.q: When is Ray 3.2 the strongest choice?
faqs.a: Use it when you already have a usable source video and need to change the visual treatment, guide specific moments, or create new delivery ratios. For native soundtracks, dialogue or lip sync, choose an audio-capable engine instead.

faqs.q: Does the page expose HDR or EXR options?
faqs.a: No. Public marketing copy stays on supported generation settings until any advanced options are product-approved.
Displayed examples use MaxVideoAI pricing with margin. The exact app quote appears before generation.
No. Treat Ray 3.2 renders as silent video outputs and add voice, music or sound design later.

custom.heroSupportLine: Best for source-video Modify, guide/keyframe edits and delivery Reframe.
custom.heroDesc1: Ray 3.2 gives teams a current Luma route for source-video Modify and Reframe, where an approved clip can be visually changed or adapted without rebuilding the shot from scratch.
custom.heroDesc2: Use it for edit passes, keyframed visual direction, delivery-ratio variants, supporting short generation tests and 1080p checks before downstream audio or editing.
heroHighlights: Modify-first value||Use Ray 3.2 when the source timing works and the visual treatment needs to change.
Guide/keyframe direction||Guide frames and indexed keyframes help steer specific source moments.
Reframe delivery||Reframe outpaints approved clips into new aspect ratios.
Silent output||Ray 3.2 is video-only in MaxVideoAI, so audio is planned downstream.
custom.specNote: Specs describe the MaxVideoAI Ray 3.2 surface for generation, Modify and Reframe.

specSections.title: Edit and reframe surface
specSections.intro: Ray 3.2 is exposed first for source-video Modify and Reframe, with generation available as a supporting route.
items: Guide frame or indexed keyframes for directed Modify passes.
Up to 64 Modify keyframe anchors where the provider route accepts them.
Text-to-video for prompt-only silent motion tests.
Image-to-video when one opening still should anchor the clip.

specSections.title: Operational notes
items: MaxVideoAI keeps Ray 3.2 requests on supported settings.
Provider capacity can vary during peak usage.
All Ray 3.2 public modes keep the Fal-reference customer quote with margin.
Internal routing does not change the price shown to the client.

custom.whatIntro1: Start with Ray 3.2 Modify when you have a source clip, or Reframe when the source clip is already approved but needs another delivery format.
custom.whatIntro2: Use prompt-only or start-image generation as a supporting path when you still need a new silent Luma motion candidate.
whatFlowSteps: Choose Luma Ray 3.2 as the engine.
Upload one source video for Modify or Reframe.
For Modify, add a guide frame or indexed keyframes when specific moments need direction.
For Reframe, choose the target ratio and subject priority.
Set duration to 5s or 10s where the mode supports it.
Use prompt-only or a start image only when generating a new clip.
Review the exact price and generate.
custom.galleryIntro: Use examples to study Modify prompts, keyframed source-video changes, Reframe delivery setups and short supporting generation tests.
strengths: Source videos help preserve timing and camera motion for Modify.
Guide frames and keyframes are useful when specific source moments need visual direction.
Reframe is useful when one approved clip needs several delivery ratios.
Good supporting fit for short product, fashion, environment and social motion tests.
1080p is useful for final inspection of selected short clips.
boundaries: Ray 3.2 output is silent in MaxVideoAI.
It is not positioned as the best all-purpose choice when native audio, dialogue or lip sync is required.
Provider capacity can vary during peak usage.
safetyRules: No sexual content or content involving minors.
No hateful, harassing or graphic-violence requests.
Do not impersonate real people or public figures without consent.
Do not include private personal data or sensitive documents.
custom.promptingTitle: How to write a strong Ray 3.2 prompt
custom.promptingIntro: Write a single edit or reframe brief with source preservation, the requested change, camera/framing intent, duration and resolution.
custom.promptingTip: Tip: for Modify, write the preservation rule first so the prompt does not fight the source video.
promptingGlobalPrinciples: Use one edit goal per source clip.
Keep the preservation rule and the requested change separate.
Use 10s only when the action needs the extra time.
Use generation only when no source clip exists yet.
promptingEngineWhy: Source videos make Modify and Reframe practical when timing is already approved.
Guide frames and keyframes help target specific moments instead of repainting the whole clip blindly.
Short Luma generation remains useful for creating candidate clips before a later edit/reframe pass.
Resolution, duration and mode choices directly affect displayed pricing.
custom.finalPara1: Ray 3.2 is the current Luma video route in MaxVideoAI when Modify, guide/keyframe control and Reframe matter more than native audio generation.
